2604.02850.title: High-resolution probabilistic estimation of three-dimensional regional ocean dynamics from sparse surface observations
2604.02850.paper_summary: The ocean interior regulates Earth's climate but remains sparsely observed due to limited in situ measurements, while satellite observations are restricted to the surface. We present a depth-aware generative framework for reconstructing high-resolution three-dimensional ocean states from extremely sparse surface data. Our approach employs a conditional denoising diffusion probabilistic model (DDPM) trained on sea surface height and temperature observations with up to 99.9 percent sparsity, without reliance on a background dynamical model. By incorporating continuous depth embeddings, the model learns a unified vertical representation of the ocean states and generalizes to previously unseen depths. Applied to the Gulf of Mexico, the framework accurately reconstructs subsurface temperature, salinity, and velocity fields across multiple depths. Evaluations using statistical metrics, spectral analysis, and heat transport diagnostics demonstrate recovery of both large-scale circulation and multiscale variability. These results establish generative diffusion models as a scalable approach for probabilistic ocean reconstruction in data-limited regimes, with implications for climate monitoring and forecasting.
2604.02850.paper_summary_zh: 海洋內部調節地球氣候，但由於現場測量有限，觀測仍然稀疏，而衛星觀測僅限於表面。<lb/>我們提出了一種深度感知生成框架，旨在從極其稀疏的表面數據中重建高解析度的三維海洋狀態。<lb/>我們的方法採用條件去噪擴散概率模型（DDPM），該模型基於海面高度和溫度觀測進行訓練，稀疏度高達99.9％，且不依賴於背景動力學模型。<lb/>通過納入連續深度嵌入，該模型學習了海洋狀態的統一垂直表示，並能夠推廣到先前未見的深度。<lb/>應用於墨西哥灣，該框架準確重建了多個深度的次表面溫度、鹽度和速度場。<lb/>使用統計指標、頻譜分析和熱量傳輸診斷的評估顯示，恢復了大規模環流和多尺度變異性。<lb/>這些結果確立了生成擴散模型作為在數據有限的情況下進行概率海洋重建的可擴展方法，對氣候監測和預測具有重要意義。
2604.02850.author: Niloofar Asefi et.al.
2604.02850.authors: Niloofar Asefi, Tianning Wu, Ruoying He, Ashesh Chattopadhyay
